error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some error_consistency issues remain to address

naming_quality.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some naming_quality issues remain to address

low_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some low_level_elegance issues remain to address

authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some authorization_consistency issues remain to address

design_coherence.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some design_coherence issues remain to address

contract_coherence.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some contract_coherence issues remain to address

logic_clarity.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some logic_clarity issues remain to address

error_consistency.dimension_character: The error handling consistency was significantly improved. AuthCodeStore.exchangeCode() now throws IllegalArgumentException instead of returning null, aligning it with GoogleTokenVerifier.verify() and MicrosoftTokenVerifier.verifyIdToken(). AuthController.exchangeCode() now uses try-catch consistent with the Google/Microsoft flows. The filter still returns raw JSON strings but these are not public API endpoints.
error_consistency.score_rationale: Score 82: Core error contract inconsistency (null vs exception) has been resolved. AuthCodeStore now throws consistently with sibling token services. The remaining gap is the MicrosoftChallengeStore.consumeNonce() null-return contract which mirrors the resolved AuthCodeStore issue but was not changed in this batch.

naming_quality.dimension_character: The naming_quality misnomer was resolved. mergeProviderConfig is now named normalizeProviderConfig, accurately describing that it applies defaults to a partial server response. Other naming in the codebase remains strong: NormalizedIdentity, AuthenticatedUserIdentity, ProviderIdentityNormalizer, UserResponse all convey intent clearly. AuthCodeStore, MicrosoftChallengeStore are self-documenting.
naming_quality.score_rationale: Score 88: The primary naming issue was fixed. Remaining minor naming notes: microsoftClientSideDisabledResponse() uses two negatives (microsoftClient + Disabled) but is clear enough. The method applyProviderIdentityFields accurately describes setting provider fields on a user object.

low_level_elegance.dimension_character: Two improvements significantly raised low-level elegance: (1) UserController.getCurrentUser() now returns ResponseEntity<UserResponse> via a static factory method, eliminating the 10-key inline Map with repeated null-coalescing. (2) The dead null check in AuthController.cookiePath() was removed. normalizeEmail() complex boolean was decomposed into named predicates.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: Score 82: UserController and cookiePath() improvements are clean. normalizeEmail() predicate extraction clarifies intent. AuthController.verifyMicrosoftToken() null check for microsoftTokenVerifierProvider.getIfAvailable() is still present but acceptable since it handles the case where the bean is conditionally not registered.

authorization_consistency.dimension_character: The duplicated Microsoft client-side enabled check was consolidated into a microsoftClientSideDisabledResponse() private method. Both /microsoft/challenge and /microsoft/verify now call this single method. The security contract is enforced consistently: all Microsoft client-side endpoints check the feature flag at the top of the handler.
authorization_consistency.score_rationale: Score 86: Duplication removed cleanly. Both endpoints now use the private helper. The remaining gap is that the server-side Microsoft gate is handled by a separate filter (MicrosoftAuthorizationGateFilter) while the client-side gate is handled in the controller — this two-layer approach is appropriate for the different flows but could be documented more clearly.

design_coherence.dimension_character: UserService.findOrCreateUser() was refactored to extract updateExistingUser() and createNewUser() private methods, making the public method a clear orchestrator. The create-new path is now isolated from the update-existing path. AuthController remains broad (5 endpoints, 8 dependencies) but the core auth/exchange flow is now cleaner.
design_coherence.score_rationale: Score 74: UserService improvement is positive. AuthController is still too wide (unchanged) — it handles Google verify, Microsoft challenge, Microsoft verify, code exchange, and logout. The UserResponse DTO extraction shows good design. Remaining: AuthController needs splitting into MicrosoftAuthController + AuthController.

contract_coherence.dimension_character: MicrosoftChallengeStore now has comprehensive Javadoc explaining session lifecycle, single-use guarantee, TTL, and the null-return contract for consumeNonce(). AuthCodeStore.exchangeCode() Javadoc was updated to document the new throw contract. AuthenticatedUserIdentity.legacy() nullable userId is still implicit.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: Score 82: MicrosoftChallengeStore and AuthCodeStore contract documentation significantly improved. AuthCodeStore now explicitly documents the IllegalArgumentException throw. The remaining gap is AuthenticatedUserIdentity.legacy() where the nullable userId is not surfaced via a @Nullable annotation or Optional type.

logic_clarity.dimension_character: The normalizeEmail() complex boolean was decomposed into three named predicates: hasExactlyOneAt, hasNoSpaces, and hasNonEmptyLocalAndDomain. This makes the validation intent clear without requiring the reader to parse a 5-clause boolean. AuthController.cookiePath() dead null check was removed.
logic_clarity.score_rationale: Score 84: normalizeEmail() is now readable without mental parsing. The dead null check in cookiePath() is gone. Remaining minor clarity gap: MicrosoftChallengeStore.consumeNonce() null return in AuthController is checked with if (expectedNonce == null) which is still a null-sentinel pattern — could be improved by throwing.

issues.summary: MicrosoftChallengeStore.consumeNonce() still returns null for invalid sessions, while AuthCodeStore now throws
issues.suggestion: Consider updating consumeNonce() to throw IllegalArgumentException on invalid/expired challenges, consistent with the updated AuthCodeStore pattern. Update AuthController to use try-catch.
evidence: MicrosoftChallengeStore.consumeNonce() Javadoc: 'Returns null if the session does not exist, has expired, or does not match'
AuthController.verifyMicrosoftToken() checks: if (expectedNonce == null) { return ResponseEntity.badRequest()... }
AuthCodeStore.exchangeCode() now throws IllegalArgumentException — consumeNonce() is still null-returning

issues.summary: applyProviderIdentityFields() verb 'apply' is vague; 'setProviderFields' or 'populateFromIdentity' would be clearer
issues.suggestion: Rename to setProviderFieldsFrom(User user, NormalizedIdentity identity) or populateFromIdentity(User user, NormalizedIdentity identity).
evidence: private static void applyProviderIdentityFields(User user, NormalizedIdentity identity)

issues.summary: microsoftTokenVerifierProvider.getIfAvailable() null check in AuthController could be abstracted
issues.suggestion: Consider extracting a getMicrosoftTokenVerifier() helper that throws or returns an Optional, reducing the null-check inline in the endpoint method.

issues.summary: The distinction between server-side Microsoft gate (filter) vs client-side gate (controller) is not documented
issues.suggestion: Add a class-level comment to AuthController explaining that client-side Microsoft gates are enforced in-controller while server-side gates are enforced by MicrosoftAuthorizationGateFilter.

issues.summary: AuthController still handles 5 distinct flows and injects 8 dependencies
issues.suggestion: Extract MicrosoftAuthController with /microsoft/challenge and /microsoft/verify endpoints and the session management helpers (currentChallengeSessionId, createChallengeSessionCookie, cookiePath). Keep AuthController for /google/verify, /exchange, /logout.
evidence: 8 constructor parameters: googleTokenVerifier, userService, jwtTokenService, authCodeStore, providerIdentityNormalizer, microsoftChallengeStore, microsoftTokenVerifierProvider, rolloutProperties, servletContextPath

issues.summary: AuthenticatedUserIdentity.legacy() has implicit nullable userId not annotated or wrapped in Optional
issues.suggestion: Add a Javadoc @param note that userId is nullable in legacy mode, or use Optional<Long> for the userId component.
evidence: public static AuthenticatedUserIdentity legacy(String email, Long userId) — userId can be null
identity.userId() is called without null check in legacy mode consumers

issues.summary: consumeNonce() returns null and AuthController checks if (expectedNonce == null) — remaining null-sentinel after AuthCodeStore was fixed to throw
issues.suggestion: Update MicrosoftChallengeStore.consumeNonce() to throw IllegalArgumentException, consistent with the updated AuthCodeStore contract.

add.description: The AuthCodeStore.exchangeCode() null-return inconsistency was fixed. Both RedisAuthCodeStore and InMemoryAuthCodeStore now throw, and AuthController uses try-catch.
The primary naming issue was fixed. normalizeProviderConfig accurately describes applying defaults to a partial server response.

add.header: UserController returns typed UserResponse DTO instead of inline Map
add.description: UserResponse record with static from(User) factory eliminates 10 inline Map entries and repeated null-coalescing from the controller method.
servletContextPath is guaranteed non-null by constructor; the null check was dead code and has been removed.

add.header: Microsoft client-side feature flag check deduplicated via private method
add.description: microsoftClientSideDisabledResponse() private method replaces the two identical inline ResponseEntity.status(503) blocks in /challenge and /verify endpoints.

add.header: UserService.findOrCreateUser() extracted into updateExistingUser() and createNew
add.description: findOrCreateUser() is now a clean 5-line orchestrator delegating to private methods for each code path.
Session lifecycle, single-use guarantee, TTL, and null-return semantics are now documented.
Updated to document IllegalArgumentException throw for invalid/expired codes.

add.header: normalizeEmail() validation decomposed into named predicates
add.description: hasExactlyOneAt, hasNoSpaces, hasNonEmptyLocalAndDomain replace a 5-clause boolean expression.
